title: Modafinil for excessive daytime sleepiness in narcolepsy (2015) [000]
abstract: Shift-work disorder symptoms improved on the Epworth Sleepiness Scale. Modafinil significantly reduced excessive daytime sleepiness versus placebo. Sleep-onset latency increased during the multiple sleep latency test. Patients with narcolepsy showed improved maintenance of wakefulness.

title: Modafinil and working memory under sleep deprivation (2004) [001]
abstract: Sustained attention and vigilance increased relative to placebo. Effects on executive function were modest in well-rested subjects. Psychomotor vigilance task lapses decreased after dosing. Modafinil improved working memory accuracy in sleep-deprived participants.

title: Mechanism of action of modafinil revisited (2008) [002]
abstract: Bioavailability and metabolism were characterized in healthy volunteers. Modafinil inhibits the dopamine transporter increasing extracellular dopamine. Plasma half-life supports once-daily dosing in most patients. The mechanism of action involves multiple monoaminergic systems.

title: Modafinil augmentation in major depressive disorder (2004) [003]
abstract: Depression severity decreased on augmentation versus placebo. Negative symptoms and cognition showed small improvements. Tolerability was acceptable across psychiatric populations.

title: Adverse-event profile of long-term modafinil use (2018) [004]
abstract: No evidence of physical dependence emerged over the study period. The most common adverse effects were headache and nausea. Modafinil showed lower abuse potential than amphetamine in human studies.

abstract: Histaminergic tone contributed to wake promotion. Modafinil activated orexinergic neurons in the hypothalamus.
